titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Henry C. Taylor papers
titlestmt.author: by Emma Gronbeck

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday July 20, 2023 at 03:11

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains correspondence, financial records, photographs, and other research materials documenting the creation of Henry C. Taylor's collection of 410 rare books, medieval manuscripts, maritime atlases, and incunabula on the science of navigation and history of exploration. Also included are drafts and related correspondence for several bibliographies of his collection compiled by three different bibliographers, including John S. Kebabian's The Henry C. Taylor Collection (Yale University Library, 1971), and materials related to Taylor's publication of a transcribed edition of John Rolfe's A True Relation of the State of Virginia .

bioghist.head: Henry C. Taylor
bioghist.p: Henry C. Taylor (1894-1971) was an American textile merchant, yachtsman, and collector of rare books and manuscripts. Born in Brooklyn, New York, Taylor graduated from Yale University in 1917 and served in the Navy during both World Wars as a lieutenant and then as a lieutenant commander. He became president of Taylor, Pinkham & Co. in 1945 and director of J.P. Stevens & Co. in 1952. He lived in Cold Spring Harbor, New York, for much of his life.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains correspondence, financial records, photographs, and other research materials documenting the creation of Henry C. Taylor's collection of 410 rare books, medieval manuscripts, maritime atlases, and incunabula on the science of navigation and history of exploration. Also included are drafts and related correspondence for several bibliographies of his collection compiled by three different bibliographers, including John S. Kebabian's The Henry C. Taylor Collection (Yale University Library, 1971), and materials related to Taylor's publication of a transcribed edition of John Rolfe's A True Relation of the State of Virginia .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Correspondence and Financial Papers, 1938-1971. II. Bibliographies, 1965-1971.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit. Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing. This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.
An additional 0.5 linear feet of materials was added to the collection during processing, in October 2021.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Henry C. Taylor Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Henry C. Taylor Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Rare printed material and autograph manuscripts bequeathed with these papers are described in John S. Kebabian, "The Henry C. Taylor Collection" (New Haven, 1971). After receipt, the manuscripts and printed items were separated from the papers for individual cataloging. Records for the 14 manuscripts can be retrieved by searching the library's online catalog for the phrase: Bequest of Henry C Taylor. Records for 396 printed items can be retrieved by searching the library's online catalog for local call numbers Taylor 15 through Taylor 410.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence between Henry C. Taylor and various colleagues and rare book dealers discussing the acquisition and provenance of rare books, atlases, medieval manuscripts, and incunabula. Also included are invoices and receipts for manuscript purchases, research materials and bibliographic records, photographs, and several files related to a transcribed and edited edition of John Rolfe's A True Relation of the State of Virginia , published by Taylor.

did.unittitle: Wroth, Lawrence C. (The John Carter Brown Library)
General correspondence by author or title

Greepe, Thomas, The True and Perfecte Newes of the Woorthy and Valiaunt Exploytes, Performed and Doone by That Valiant Knight Syr Frauncis Drake: Not Onely at Sancto Domingo, and Carthagena, But Also Nowe at Cales, and Vppon the Coast of Spaine, 1587 , edited by David W. Waters, draft, typescript, with correspondence
Manuscript photostats and photographs, with correspondence
Rolfe, John, A True Relation of the State of Virginia lefte by Sir Thomas Dale, Kt., in May Last, 1616 , print edition edited by Henry C. Taylor
Gift copy mailing list and thank-you letters
A Splendid Portolan Atlas by Francesco Ghisolfi of Genoa in a Magnificent Luxuriously Decorated Mid-Sixteenth Century Binding with the Combined Arms of the Medici and Austria , manuscript description

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of correspondence, drafts, page proofs, and research materials for several bibliographies of the Henry C. Taylor Collection, compiled by John S. Kebabian, Rivan Alkemade, and Louis H. Silverstein.

did.unittitle: Bibliography of atlases, incunabula, and manuscripts, 1596-1610, compiled by [John S. Kebabian?], drafts, typescript
Bibliography of atlases, incunabula, and manuscripts, 1611-1631, compiled by [John S. Kebabian?], drafts, typescript
"H. C. Taylor Collection, Descriptions of Atlases" by Rivan Alkemade, draft, typescript
Kebabian, John S., The Henry C. Taylor Collection
